# Bounded Attention

## Explains

Systems must be designed to show bounded windows of 3-4 levels rather than
infinite depth, because better VIEWS solve complexity better than simpler SYSTEMS.

## The Concept

Human cognition holds approximately four chunks simultaneously (Miller's law,
adjusted by subsequent research). You cannot see eight levels of hierarchy at
once and make good decisions. The information is there, but your ability to
reason about it simultaneously is not. This is a hard constraint, not a training
problem.

The solution isn't flatter systems — it's better views of deep systems. The
framework's lens model (anchor at any node, see L0-L3, re-anchor to go deeper)
directly implements this insight. You don't need to see everything; you need to
see the right 3-4 levels for the current decision.

Governance surfaces, dashboards, reports, and navigation must all respect bounded
attention. Show the relevant context window, not everything. A venture dashboard
doesn't show individual task status. A task view doesn't show portfolio strategy.
Each view is complete for its purpose and bounded by design.

Complexity isn't the enemy; unmanaged exposure to complexity is. A system can be
arbitrarily deep and still be navigable if every view respects the 3-4 level
window. The discipline is in the presentation layer, not the data layer.

## Why This Matters for Governance

JOS governs objects across multiple hierarchy levels — from meta-principles down
to individual venture rules. Without bounded attention as a design principle,
any attempt to navigate this system would overwhelm the user. The lens model,
registry views, and charter abstractions all exist to present bounded windows
into a complex underlying structure.

This also explains why documentation must be layered — summaries link to details,
details link to specifications. No single document tries to contain everything.
Each document respects the reader's attention budget.

## Connects To

- JOS-C24 (Cognitive Load Budgeting)
- JOS-C14 (Ambient Discoverability)
- Framework §2-3 (lenses and principles)

## Provenance

Framework side-quest lens model foundation — universalized from eng-domain
JOS-C24.

### Framework Connection

Framework §2 (lens tiers bounding attention to L0-L3 per view), §3 (3-4 levels per view as a structural constraint), and §4 (universal level pattern) directly implement bounded attention — every navigation surface respects the cognitive window.
